_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
reference_document_element_description: Skill in formulating business problems as machine learning problems, including defining the prediction target, identifying appropriate inputs, and establishing measurable success criteria.
comments: NICE skill intersects with WIDAI AI domain. Skill AI-S-001 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: DG-S-009 (DG, cosine: 0.517), LS-S-009 (LS, cosine: 0.480).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark. Both texts processed jointly through transformer; outputs calibrated 0-1 similarity score.
primary_method.significance: STS 6.4/10 (raw 0.641). Shared vocabulary: [skill]. NICE-only terms [forecasting, requirements] vs WIDAI-only terms [appropriate, business, criteria, defining, establishing, formulating] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.description: Token-level contextual embedding matching. Precision = FDE coverage of KSA tokens. Recall = KSA coverage by FDE tokens. F1 = harmonic mean.
bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.877 (P=0.905, R=0.850). P exceeds R by 0.055 — the NICE element's 4 words are well-captured by the WIDAI KSA's 22 words, but the KSA contains additional specificity not reflected in the NICE element.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference. Classifies text pair as entailment, neutral, or contradiction. Indicates directional implication strength.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.6%). "Skill in forecasting requirements" and "Skill in formulating business problems as machine learning problems, including d" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity. Each text encoded independently; measures vector-space proximity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.518 < STS 0.641 (gap -0.123). Cross-attention finds deeper alignment than independent encoding. The relationship depends on cross-text reasoning — concept-level mapping between [forecasting, requirements] and [appropriate, business, criteria] that bi-encoders structurally cannot capture.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens). Purely lexical baseline, hand-traceable.
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 8.7%. Shared tokens: [skill]. NICE-only: [forecasting, requirements]. WIDAI-only: [appropriate, business, criteria, defining, establishing, formulating].
